content:
And while I’m willing to change some aspects of the script due to budgetary concerns, I will be less willing changing much of the script regarding character and character development, or plot. After consulting with a few of my peers whose opinions I highly value, not just regarding writing, but writing for film as well, they gave me some feedback and I took that into account while drafting the first draft.

I’m afraid that a lot of the suggestions you’ve offered me makes me think that you don’t at all understand at all the film that I’ve wrote. And it also makes me think that you’ve written off my ideas because they don’t immediately align with your ideas. For instance, you suggesting I change Sheen’s job; this is such an important aspect of the entire film because El is an artist, and this is how she relates to Sheen. Artist’s have a darkness they need to conquer or live with, and this is important for El to understand because of her own dark path. The whole crux of the film works on Sheen losing out on the opportunity for her to achieve her dreams because she couldn’t control her dark past (the last scene). I am writing a character study of a strong and powerful young female lead who finds her voice.
